acceptance.assertion: Deployment drain coordinates request admission and dispatcher workers so normal connector traffic cannot indefinitely replenish worker occupancy after drain begins.
Cutover occurs only after queued and in-flight work are observably quiescent for the required consecutive samples; no unsafe worker killing or occupancy spoofing is introduced.
Failure to quiesce still aborts deployment and preserves the previous healthy runtime with a valid failure/finalization receipt.
Tests reproduce the observed queue=0 with nonzero dispatcher_worker_pool_occupancy scenario and prove a deploy can converge without requiring the operator to stop using the connector manually.
An exact source-identity-bound deployment passes with normal connector conditions and independent runtime identity and health readback.

approval.note: Harden deploy/drain coordination without weakening the existing fail-closed rollback boundary.

goal: Remove the operational dependency on a manually quiet connector window during source-identity-bound runtime deployments while preserving fail-closed drain semantics and rollback when queued or in-flight work cannot be safely quiesced.

metadata.live_basis: Exact deploy job grabowski-job-7cf2257513c1 failed safely with finalization receipt SHA-256 f977a20205bca51bd17b144b6701625f79faaa1f96c91b86ca581a00458be51c after queue reached zero but dispatcher_worker_pool_occupancy remained nonzero. An identical retry succeeded when connector traffic was kept quiet during drain.

title: Make runtime deploy drain coordinate with active connector workers
